headline: Is Tesla (TSLA) Pricing In Too Much Future Growth After Recent 1-Year Surge?
summary: If you are wondering whether Tesla's share price reflects its true worth today, you are not alone. This article focuses squarely on what that current price might mean for value oriented investors. Tesla recently closed at US$392.50, with returns of 11.4% over 7 days, 6.7% over 30 days, a 10.4% decline year to date, and gains of 72.5% over 1 year, 141.5% over 3 years, and 59.5% over 5 years. This gives a wide range of outcomes for anyone thinking about timing an entry or exit. Recent...

headline: This Week In Energy Transition - CIP Advances Major Energy Storage Project in Chile

headline: Obi Releases New Data On Tesla Robotaxi Pricing In San Francisco
summary: Tesla's Robotaxi service remains the least expensive ride-hailing option for travelers in San Francisco, but the average price of a ride has risen 41% since December according to new research from Obi, the global real-time aggregator for rideshare prices. Despite the sharp rise, Tesla remains the lowest-priced rideshare option across the market, underscoring its continued role as a disruptive force in autonomous mobility.

content.title: Obi Releases New Data On Tesla Robotaxi Pricing In San Francisco
content.summary: Tesla's Robotaxi service remains the least expensive ride-hailing option for travelers in San Francisco, but the average price of a ride has risen 41% since December according to new research from Obi, the global real-time aggregator for rideshare prices. Despite the sharp rise, Tesla remains the lowest-priced rideshare option across the market, underscoring its continued role as a disruptive force in autonomous mobility.

thumbnail.caption: Obi is the world's largest rideshare price and ETA aggregator.

headline: Apple's Tim Cook will still be around to help with China
summary: Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook announced on Monday that he plans to step down as chief executive role, effective September 1, and transition to an executive chairman role. Cook will be succeeded by Apple senior vice president of hardware engineering John Ternus. LightShed Partners partner and TMT analyst Walter Piecyk comes on Morning Brief to discuss what this change may mean for Apple's mission and the iPhone maker's relationship with China.

content.title: Apple's Tim Cook will still be around to help with China
content.description:
Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook announced on Monday that he plans to step down as chief executive role, effective September 1, and transition to an executive chairman role. Cook will be succeeded by Apple senior vice president of hardware engineering John Ternus.

LightShed Partners partner and TMT analyst Walter Piecyk comes on Morning Brief to discuss what this change may mean for Apple's mission and the iPhone maker's relationship with China.
content.summary: Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook announced on Monday that he plans to step down as chief executive role, effective September 1, and transition to an executive chairman role. Cook will be succeeded by Apple senior vice president of hardware engineering John Ternus. LightShed Partners partner and TMT analyst Walter Piecyk comes on Morning Brief to discuss what this change may mean for Apple's mission and the iPhone maker's relationship with China.
